message:
fix(fnp-python): count_nonzero + fix_invalid fill_value conventions

Two HIGH regressions caught in review cycle 11:

1. count_nonzero(arr, axis=None) returned Python int via .item().
   numpy.count_nonzero returns a numpy.int64 (0-D scalar) — repr
   'np.int64(3)' not '3'. Removed the .item() call so the result
   stays as a 0-D numpy scalar; extract via get_item(()) which
   preserves numpy dtype.

   Asymmetry with ma_count (fixed in 45d81cb which DOES use .item()):
   numpy.ma.count returns Python int for axis=None (masked-array
   convention), while numpy.count_nonzero returns numpy.int64
   (non-masked ufunc convention). Each wrapper now follows its
   upstream's convention.

2. fix_invalid(a, fill_value=F) was setting result.fill_value=F.
   numpy's semantics: F is a REPLACEMENT value for invalid entries
   in .data, NOT a value for the output's .fill_value attribute.
   The output's fill_value is inherited from the input MaskedArray
   or left at the dtype default. Only propagate fill_value when
   input was already masked (preserves caller's fill_value). User
   code calling fix_invalid(a, fill_value=-99) now gets the numpy
   default 1e+20 on the output's fill_value attribute, same as
   numpy.ma.fix_invalid.

Tests resolved:
  tests::count_nonzero_matches_numpy_axis_none
  tests::count_nonzero_matches_numpy_keepdims_none_and_scalar_inputs
  tests::fix_invalid_matches_numpy_across_mask_copy_fill_and_shapes

Still open from cycle 11:
  tests::frompyfunc_live_callable_matches_numpy_reduce_scalar_where_and_direct_out
  — error-text drift ('output must be an array' vs 'return arrays
  must be of ArrayType'). Error TYPE matches (TypeError both sides);
  text differs. Filing as separate MEDIUM bead.

Verified via cargo test -p fnp-python --lib:
  count_nonzero_matches_numpy_axis_none:              1 ok / 0 fail
  count_nonzero_matches_numpy_keepdims_...:            1 ok / 0 fail
  fix_invalid_matches_numpy_across_mask_copy_...:     1 ok / 0 fail

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
